title: narcissus linnaeanus subsp. ochroleucus
intro: Narcissus linnaeanus subsp. ochroleucus, also known as the yellow narcissus, is a subspecies of flowering bulbous perennial plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and strap-like leaves. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its alkaloids, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Narcissus linnaeanus subsp. ochroleucus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
